subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Define life processes and identify essential ones for survival
Understand the basic difference between Autotrophic and Heterotrophic nutrition
Identify the organs involved in human digestion, respiration, and excretion
Explain the process of photosynthesis in simple terms
Recognize the role of blood and the heart in transportation
Understand the basic purpose of excretion in living organisms

lesson_plan.title: What are Life Processes? & Nutrition
teaching_strategy: Brainstorming: What makes something 'alive'?
Visual aids showing a green plant vs. a cow eating grass
Real-life examples of parasites and saprophytes
student_activities: Categorize a list of organisms as Autotrophs or Heterotrophs
Observe a potted plant and discuss how it 'eats'
assessment: Matching activity: Organism to Nutrition type

lesson_plan.title: Photosynthesis: How Plants Make Food
teaching_strategy: Diagram of a leaf cross-section on the board
Simple word equation for photosynthesis
Stomata 'Open/Close' hand-gesture analogy
student_activities: Draw and label a simple diagram of a plant taking in $CO_{2}$ and releasing $O_{2}$
assessment: Notebook checking of the leaf diagram
List the three main events of photosynthesis

lesson_plan.title: Nutrition in Humans
topics_covered: Basic role of Saliva, Stomach acid, and Bile
teaching_strategy: Human digestive system chart demonstration
Storytelling: The journey of a 'Bite of Apple'
Using a sponge to demonstrate absorption by Villi
student_activities: Label a pre-drawn outline of the digestive system
Discuss why we should chew food properly
assessment: Quick-fire round: Which organ produces bile?
Short answer: What is the role of the stomach?

lesson_plan.title: Respiration
teaching_strategy: Breathing exercise: Deep inhale/exhale to feel the chest move
Balloon model of lungs demonstration
Discussion on why we get cramps after running
student_activities: Trace the path of air from nostrils to lungs
assessment: Fill in the blanks: Energy is released in the form of _____
Exit slip: Name the respiratory pigment in our blood

lesson_plan.title: Transportation in Humans
topics_covered: The Heart as a pump (4 chambers)
teaching_strategy: Feeling the pulse at the wrist
Color-coded heart diagram (Oxygenated vs. Deoxygenated)
Analogy: Heart as a water pump and Blood vessels as pipes
student_activities: Identify the function of Platelets (clotting) through a story of a knee scrap
Color a heart diagram using Red and Blue pencils
assessment: Oral quiz: Which blood cells fight germs?

lesson_plan.title: Transportation in Plants & Excretion
topics_covered: Excretion in humans (Kidneys and Nephrons - basic)
How plants get rid of waste
teaching_strategy: Mnemonic: **X**ylem for **W**ater, **P**hloem for **F**ood
Chart showing the Human Excretory System
Discussing why we sweat and urinate
student_activities: List 3 ways plants excrete (leaf fall, resin, etc.)
Identify the bean-shaped organ in the human body
assessment: Identifying the main waste product removed by kidneys

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Assessment
teaching_strategy: Chapter mind-map on the board
Interactive 'Organ System' quiz
Doubt clearing round

teaching_aids: Human Anatomy Charts (Digestive, Respiratory, Circulatory, Excretory)
Balloon and bottle lung demonstration kit
teacher_tip: Since 'Life Processes' is a long chapter, focus on one system at a time and use body-based analogies (like the pulse or breathing) to keep students engaged with their own biology.
